subject: Physics
title: Gravitational Waves Detected
description: A micro-lesson that makes Gravitational Waves Detected usable.

hook.text: In 2015, LIGO detected ripples in spacetime caused by two black holes merging 1.3 billion years ago.

buildup.text: Einstein predicted gravitational waves in 1916. They're created when massive objects accelerate violently.

discovery.text: LIGO measured a change in arm length of one-thousandth the width of a proton. The most precise measurement ever.

twist.text: The two black holes were 29 and 36 solar masses. Their merger released more power than all visible stars combined.

climax.text: Kip Thorne, Rainer Weiss, and Barry Barish won the 2017 Nobel Prize for this detection.

punchline.text: Spacetime ripples from colliding black holes. Heard across a billion light-years.

quiz.question: What caused the gravitational waves detected by LIGO in 2015?
options: Two black holes merging 1.3 billion light-years away
A supernova explosion in our galaxy
The Big Bang's echo reaching Earth
